title: galium pumilum subsp. normanii
intro: Galium pumilum subsp. normanii, also known as Norman's Bedstraw, is a flowering plant species within the Rubiaceae family, characterized by its tiny white or pinkish flowers and oval-shaped leaves. Traditionally, Norman's Bedstraw has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it useful in treating various health issues. The plant's health benefits are attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation, ease digestive problems, and support immune function, while its herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, are often consumed to take advantage of these benefits. However, due to the lack of extensive research, its effectiveness and safety as a medicinal plant should be verified by a healthcare professional before use.
